why: Every thermal candidate needs source-envelope pass before release
acceptance: At least one candidate row has strict source-envelope pass with legal source/property labels.

why: Re Pr Gz enthalpy and storage need property release
acceptance: cp/mu/k source paths validity intervals and split permission pass.

why: Qwall/exchange candidates need same-QOI mesh/time support
acceptance: Formal GCI or explicit fail-closed disposition exists for exact QOIs.

why: Pressure candidates need endpoint fields after terminal handoff
acceptance: Terminal success/failure and endpoint fields are documented.

why: Protected scoring requires one frozen runtime-legal candidate
acceptance: One candidate manifest fixes inputs coefficients and split policy before scoring.
